free_text: -Capture cross sections and gamma-ray spectra from
the interaction of 0.5 to 3.0 MeV neutron with
nuclei in the mass range A=63 to 209 .
For energy range 1-3 MeV, resolution +-67 keV
Tritium targets of 0.8 to 1.2 mg/cm**2 tritium
adsorbed in titanium deposited on gold backing.
Metallic Li evaporated onto thick tantalum backing.
Proton beam pulsed at 10 nsec, repetition rate 2.5 MHz,
bunched to about 1 nsec. Mean current intensity
Neutron energy spread corresponding to 1 mg/cm**2
tritium target +- 55.keV at 1 MeV, +-33 keV at 3.MeV.
Neutron flux with energy spread between 40 and 80 keV.
Prompt gamma ray technique with extrapolation
of the pulse height spectrum to zero energy by means
NaI scintillator for prompt gammas in anti-
long NaI scintillator surrounded by an annular NaI
crystal.Energy resolution for E-gamma=1.836 MeV was
8.5% in anti-Compton mode, 12% in first-escape mode.
Two BF3 counters for neutron fluence detection.
50 or 60 mm diameter disks of 1.5 to 16 mm thick.
at 0 deg and 6 cm from lithium or tritium target.
-For gamma ray attenuation in the samples
-For neutron attenuation in the sample and in the air
Efficiency of gamma ray spectrometer error 6.%
Error of the neutron long counter
Error of the gamma ray attenuation in
Error due to the neutron source
Error due to extrapolation of gamma
Total relative error on the capture x-sect 7.0 -31.0
Data taken from reference and from the author.

Cross section for equivalent natural element was
deduced using the isotopic abundances and measured
cross sections of separate isotopes. Then measured
cross section and deduced cross section were combined
to reduce the uncertainty in final result.
